meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Scaling Projects for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome project scaling hurdles with the Antigravity project template. A beginner-friendly guide to building professional SaaS apps using Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves scaling bottlenecks by providing a production-ready, modular architecture. By pairing this scaffold with Claude Code, students can automate boilerplate, enforce consistent design patterns, and rapidly iterate on features, transforming fragmented code into a robust, scalable SaaS foundation in minutes.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting your first app, but as the feature list grows, the codebase has morphed into a fragile, tangled mess. Every attempt to add a simple button triggers a cascade of bugs, and the dream of a 'scalable project' feels like a distant technical mirage. You aren't failing; you're just hitting the wall where amateur coding ends and professional architecture begins.

Today, we shatter that wall. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain access to the same structural integrity used by senior engineers. Combined with the power of Claude Code, you will stop fighting your own code and start building with the velocity of a seasoned architect. Let’s turn that chaos into a clean, scalable system.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a production-grade directory structure that separates concerns, ensuring your UI, business logic, and API routes don't bleed into each other.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity base template, including modular folder structure for components, services, and hooks.

steps.title: Define Domain Entities
steps.description: Use Antigravity's type-safety patterns to define your data models early, preventing the common 'undefined' errors that plague beginner projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create TypeScript interfaces for our primary entities and generate the corresponding validation schemas.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate models for User and Project entities using Zod"

steps.title: Modularize Business Logic
steps.description: Move hardcoded logic out of UI components and into reusable service layers to keep your codebase clean and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current inline logic into a dedicated service layer following the Antigravity modular pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "extract business logic into service/ directory"

steps.title: Implement Automated Testing Hooks
steps.description: Integrate test scaffolds early to ensure that new features don't break the existing build, a critical habit for scaling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit test boilerplates for the newly created services using the Antigravity test configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate unit tests for all files in services/"

steps.title: Deploy the Scalable Foundation
steps.description: Perform a final environment audit to ensure your production configuration is ready for real-world traffic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the project configuration for production readiness, focusing on environment variables and performance optimizations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "check config and optimize build pipeline"

pro_tips: Treat your 'services' folder as the source of truth, never let UI components handle data fetching directly.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation comments for every function automatically; it pays dividends during debugging.
Small commits are better than big merges; push your code every time a single Antigravity module is completed.
Ignore 'perfect code' early on; focus on the Antigravity structure first, then use Claude Code to refactor for performance later.
Always run the linter before and after every AI-assisted change to maintain code hygiene.
content.next_steps: Once you have your foundation, start implementing a single 'CRUD' feature using the patterns established in your template to solidify your understanding of the architecture.
content.conversion_block: You now have the structural blueprint to build beyond the beginner trap. Take these lessons, apply the Antigravity workflow, and watch your velocity skyrocket. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
